raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Without a sustainable source of funding, budget challenges exacerbated by the pandemic will force transit agencies across the Bay Area to make major service cuts.",
    "BART could be forced to cut 65-85% of service, meaning trains only every 60 minutes, no weekend service, and station closures and line shutdowns.",
    "MUNI could face a 50% reduction in frequency on lines, no regular service after 9 p.m., and the elimination of Muni fare subsidies for low-income riders, seniors, and youth.",
    "Major cuts to public transportation service would cause ripple effects for everyone in the Bay Area.",
    "Commutes could increase by up to 10 hours per week for key routes connecting the East Bay to San Francisco.",
    "Traffic on the Bay Bridge could increase 72%.",
    "In San Francisco alone, the 13,000 students who ride MUNI could lose their route to school.",
    "Public transportation in the Bay Area has long been underfunded compared to other jurisdictions.",
    "Crime on BART is down 17% year-over-year.",
    "MUNI earned its highest rider satisfaction rating in 20 years after cutting major subway delays 76% and short delays down 89%."
  ]
}
```
